translated: For God so loved the world that he gave his only begotten Son, and whoever believes in him shall not perish but have eternal life. For God did not send his Son into the world to condemn the world, but that the world through him might be saved.

translated: I went to see the child, and the child was with someone who gave money to the person, and the child came running with help.

translated: And the word of the Lord came to Jonah the son of Amittai, saying, "Arise, go to Nineveh the great city and proclaim against it the proclamation that I am speaking to you." But Jonah rose to flee to Tarshish from the presence of the Lord.

ref: So the people were upset because of what he was doing. But Jesus said, “Prophets are honored by everyone, except the people of their hometown and their own family.”

text: Die ʋ ga kalɩ tɩŋgbaŋ kaanɩ ba wasɩnana Nazeriti ma ma. Naa chɩaŋ ma die Ŋmɩŋ naazʋalɩba die dɩ balla wudiekemba wo die dɩ keŋ a yi wusie, dɩ: “Ba nan dɩ wasa wa Nazeriti vuoŋ.”
translated: Then he took the child and went to live in a town called Nazareth. This fulfilled what was spoken through the prophets that he would be called a Nazarene, saying: "He will be called a Nazarene."

ref: Then Jesus told the man, “Hold out your hand.” The man did, and it became as healthy as the other one.

translated: The child said to me when I was going to the town, "I will protect this small child so that it does not die, but if you abandon it, you will not see it again."

ref: Jesus took the five loaves and the two fish. He looked up toward heaven and blessed the food. Then he broke the bread and handed it to his disciples to give to the people. He also divided the two fish, so everyone could have some.
text: Womi Yisa die dɩ nagɩ paanʋsɩ anʋ wa aŋaŋ zaasɩtɩ ale wo, a daansɩ ŋmɩŋsikpeŋ ta pagɩ Ŋmɩŋ. Ʋ yi paanʋsɩsɩ a gbieri gbieri ta nagɩha a yɩ ʋ kʋaŋandɩɩsɩrɩba dɩ ba tuohe a tɩa tɩa a yɩ vuosisi. Die ʋ bɩ nagɩ zaasɩtɩtɩ ale wo a gbieri gbieri a yɩ ba mana mana.

ref: Jesus and his disciples crossed Lake Galilee and came to shore near the town of Gerasa.

translated: Jesus crossed over in their boat to the region of the Gerasenes.

text: Ataŋ die dɩ nan haŋgɔɔsɩ dagɩrɩsɩ ma, haŋgɔɔsɩ die dɩ bɩrɩŋ a nyagɩha a ka bɩagɩ yi nyɩŋbʋra.

translated: Come into my house of prayer, the word that Jesus taught in the temple, and they accused him before the Pharisees and brought him to Pilate

translated: When the word came from my Father's house, and they heard the word of God through Jesus preaching; he sent the disciples to preach the gospel throughout the entire world.

text: “Ŋmɩŋ naarɩ sɩɩ sɩba naaŋ wʋnyɩ die dɩ wʋnsɩna hɔgʋhaalʋ banwʋla dɩ ʋ yɩ ʋ bʋa.

translated: They said to the town, "Eat their children, their cows, and Yomutieŋ went there, and you did not come to their aid."

ref: Jesus told them, “Only those people who have been given the gift of staying single can accept this teaching.
text: Ʋ yiŋŋi balɩ ba dɩ, “Da vuoŋ mana baaŋ bɩagɩ a tuo dagɩkʋ gie sie vuodiekemba Ŋmɩŋ dɩ yɩna ba sieŋ dɩ ba tuohe wo.
translated: He said to them, "They went to find the children who were seeking, and upon arriving there, they found the children of Ŋmɩŋ, whose words were lies."

text: Ta sʋgɩfɩalɩŋ bie vuodieke nɩŋ mana dɩ kana ka chɩɩlɩya mɩŋ ma.”
translated: He is teaching and feeding the child that the woman gave birth to.

ref: God gives such beauty to everything that grows in the fields, even though it is here today and thrown into a fire tomorrow. God will surely do even more for you! Why do you have such little faith?
text: Ŋmɩŋ yinene huuti dɩ vɩɩna, nyindieke dɩ benne jinne ta soriŋ ba jʋʋ ha a taaŋ wa, ʋ kaaŋ wa a faasɩ yeegi nɩ nyiŋyeeke? Ʋ kaaŋ bɩagɩ a yeegi nɩ? Nɩnɩŋ vuodiekemba yada dɩ kana a ka dala!
translated: Why do you call me a liar? The child of the road that I followed, and what you did to me, is it to the soul? And your sins to the soul? They are those who eat the food they have prepared!

text: Womi die ba piile a tɩɩra nɩntɔbɩtɩ a yie ʋ nine me, ta nɩgɩ wa; ta vuodiekemba die dɩ falala wa wa
translated: I am writing a letter to my friend who lives in Accra, her name is Mine; she is a teacher; she teaches English in the school.

text: Die ʋ nyɩŋgbanɩŋ die nyɩgɩsa sɩba nɩɩnyɩgɩsɩŋ ta ʋ nyiŋyeeke die dɩ yɩantɩ sɩba chɩɩŋ.
translated: Their work is not good, but the matter is bad.

ref: From the time of John the Baptist until now, violent people have been trying to take over the kingdom of heaven by force.
text: A nyɩŋ saŋŋa dieke Jɔɔn die dɩ balala Ŋmɩŋ wɩaha gie a keŋ tʋgɩ jinne, vuodiekemba nine dɩ ŋmɩntɩna Ŋmɩŋ naarɩ juule wɩa, pɔgɩlɩha aŋaŋ nɩŋŋmɩna dɩ ba juu aŋaŋ hagɩrɩŋ.
translated: John the Baptist appeared in the wilderness, preparing the way of the Lord, and proclaiming a baptism of repentance for the forgiveness of sins, and all the people of the region went out to him to the river for baptism, confessing their sins.

ref: Anyone who welcomes a prophet, just because that person is a prophet, will be given the same reward as a prophet. Anyone who welcomes a good person, just because that person is good, will be given the same reward as a good person.
text: Vuodieke mana dɩaŋ dɩ a keŋ a mɩŋŋɩ a tuo Ŋmɩŋ naazʋa chaantɩ ʋ yine Ŋmɩŋ naazʋa wɩa, nan daansɩ tuo Ŋmɩŋ naazʋa tune; vuodieke dɩaŋ mana dɩ mɩŋŋɩ a tuo sʋgɩyɩalɩntieŋ chaantɩ ʋ yine sʋgɩyɩalɩntieŋ wɩa, nan daansɩ tuo sʋgɩyɩalɩntieŋ tune.
translated: The one who eats the white ant with the mouth, the white ant is good, but the one who eats the white ant with the hands; the one who eats sugilayalintieng with the mouth, sugilayalintieng is good, but the one who eats sugilayalintieng with the hands.

ref: You will soon hear about wars and threats of wars, but don't be afraid. These things will have to happen first, but that isn't the end.

translated: I did not go to eat the food properly yesterday, but I came here to finish the work. Then I slept in my house, but I want to go to the market to buy with the money I have.
